[[Michael Collins (footballer, born 1986)|Michael Collins]] (cousin) | education = }} }} '''Simon Grix''' (born 28 September 1985) is a professional [[rugby league]] coach who is an assistant coach for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]] and a former professional rugby league footballer. He is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and was the team's manager from May 2019 to Oct 2023. He served as [[Hull FC]]'s interim coach in the [[2024 Super League season]]. ==Background== Grix was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire, England]]. ==Playing career== ===Halifax=== Grix began his career at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] spending the 2003 and 2004 seasons at the club. [[File:Simon Grix.JPG |thumb|right|Grix playing for the Warrington Wolves]] ===Warrington Wolves=== In 2006 he joined the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]]. He missed out on selection through injury for three [[Challenge Cup Final]]s.{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/whereilive/9892981.Grix_misses_final_for_the_third_time/?ref=rss|title=Simon Grix misses his third Challenge Cup Final for Warrington Wolves|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=19 February 2017|accessdate=7 July 2017}} He played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] defeat to the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|accessdate=20 November 2019}} He played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat to the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |accessdate=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archivedate=29 July 2013}}{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=October 7, 2013|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=13 November 2019}}{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=13 November 2019}}{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |accessdate=13 November 2019}} ===Halifax Panthers=== After 10 years at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]] he returned to Halifax. ===International career=== He was mooted for a call-up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]], and was named in the [[Ireland national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Training Squad|Ireland training squad]] but did not make the final side.{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/07/30/white-eyes-a-green-jersey.aspx|title=White Eyes a Green Jersey|publisher=RLWC08.com|date=2008-07-21|accessdate=2008-07-30}}{{dead link|date=June 2018|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}{{cite web|url=http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|title=Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=2008-08-01|accessdate=2008-08-02|archive-date=2020-04-14|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200414090730/http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|url-status=dead}} He eventually made his Ireland début in 2010. He was named in their squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] but was ruled out by injury prior to the tournament and was replaced by [[Matty Hadden]].{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/news/12040/8983597|title=Matty Hadden of Oxford called into Ireland squad|publisher=skysports.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}} ==Coaching career== On 22 May 2019, it was announced that Grix would become the head coach of [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] following a successful stint as interim.{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=22 May 2019|accessdate=19 Apr 2021|title=HALIFAX APPOINT SIMON GRIX|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/54963/halifax-appoint-simon-grix}} In the 2021 Championship season, Grix guided Halifax to a third placed finish which qualified them for the playoffs. After defeating Whitehaven in the first week, Halifax travelled to Featherstone with the winner to play [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Million Pound Game]]. Halifax would lose the match 42-10 which ended their season.{{cite web|url=https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-42-10-halifax-panthers-heavy-defeat-ends-halifaxs-hopes-of-reaching-million-pound-game-3405505/|title=Featherstone 42-10 Halifax Panthers: Heavy defeat ends Halifax's hopes of reaching Million Pound Game|website=www.halifaxcourier.co.uk}} He took over the role of interim head coach following the sacking of [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]] on 11 Apr 2024{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=11 Apr 2024|accessdate=21 Jul 2024|title=Tony Smith: Hull FC head coach departs after 18 months in charge|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68785750}} ==Personal life== He is the younger brother of the rugby league footballer; [[Scott Grix]], and cousin of the association footballer and manager; [[Michael Collins (footballer, born 1986)|Michael Collins]]. ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20180509220431/https://www.halifaxrlfc.co.uk/squads/first_team/player_info?522801-Simon-Grix Profile at halifaxrlfc.co.uk] *[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34261094 Simon Grix: Warrington Wolves back to join coaching staff] *[http://www.warringtonwolves.org/playerdisplayRL.ink?skip=9&season=2008&playertype=s Profile at warringtonwolves.com]{{dead link|date=January 2017|bot=InternetArchiveBot|fix-attempted=yes }} {{Hull FC squad}} {{Navboxes |title= Coaching positions |list1= {{Halifax Panthers coaches}} {{Hull FC coaches}} }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Grix, Simon}} [[Category:1985 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:English people of Irish descent]] [[Category:English rugby league coaches]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:Halifax Panthers coaches]] [[Category:Halifax Panthers players]] [[Category:Hull F.C. coaches]] [[Category:Ireland national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Halifax, West Yorkshire]] [[Category:Rugby league second-rows]] [[Category:Warrington Wolves players]]
